scenarios.scenario-1: Verification email subject clearly explains proxy access confirmation.
scenarios.scenario-2: HTML email includes CTA button, fallback link, and UTF-8-safe readable text.
scenarios.scenario-3: Templates never render mojibake or raw placeholder garbage in Russian copy.
scenarios.scenario-4: Rendered subject, HTML, and text variants never degrade into mojibake after JSON encoding.
scenarios.scenario-5: Returning users are told to trust only the freshest reissued tg://proxy link after hardening rollout.

critical-invariants.invariant-1: Subject, HTML, and text outputs are valid UTF-8 binaries.
critical-invariants.invariant-2: Russian copy remains readable after JSON encoding and provider transport.

verification-levels.module-local: Template render checks for subject, HTML, and text binaries.
verification-levels.wave-level: Adapter payload checks after JSON encoding.
verification-levels.phase-level: Delivered email remains readable in the live request flow.

evidence.item: `validate_email_template.sh` currently asserts the shipped Russian literals and payload members.
`validate_resend_adapter.sh` confirms the adapter keeps using the template builder in the outbound request path.
`validate_utf8_runtime_copy.sh` proves compiled email subject/body remain readable after beam generation.
The template verification now guards the rollout warning that only the freshest reissued link should be trusted.

production-gates.gate-1: No email variant contains mojibake for Cyrillic copy.
production-gates.gate-2: Compiled runtime probe for email subject/body stays green after every release-affecting change.
production-gates.gate-3: Email copy tells returning users that older tg://proxy links can be superseded by a fresh reissue.
